title: Unit vector
short: A vector of length 1 that records only direction: û = u/|u|.
full:
A unit vector has magnitude exactly 1, so it carries direction without any size. You build one by dividing a vector by its own magnitude — a step called normalizing. Multiplying a unit vector by a length then rebuilds a vector of any size pointing the same way.

The standard basis unit vectors i = (1,0,0), j = (0,1,0) and k = (0,0,1) point along the coordinate axes, and every vector is a combination of them.
